script: Okay so my credit card bill came last month. Forty-seven thousand rupees. I don't even know what I bought. Probably nothing. Just forty-seven thousand rupees of vibes. So I paid it on CRED. Got points. Not money — points. I was like, okay, points for what? Then I opened the CRED store. Flights. Hotels. Premium brands. With points that I got for paying a bill I was already going to pay. That's it. That's the whole thing. Also — and this is the actual feature — it sends me a reminder when my bill is due. Because I am a 33-year-old man who somehow forgets he has a credit card and then panics on the due date. Download CRED. Pay your bill. Get points. Worst case you get a reminder and don't pay a late fee. That's genuinely worth it.
hook: Okay so my credit card bill came last month. Forty-seven thousand rupees.
setup_summary: Self-deprecating about not knowing what he bought, paying late fees
product_reveal_line: So I paid it on CRED
cynicism_beat: Not money — points. I was like, okay, points for what?
cta: Download CRED. Pay your bill. Get points. Worst case you get a reminder and don't pay a late fee.
notes: Numbers make it specific. Reveal at line 3. Cynicism acknowledged immediately then resolved. CTA is practical not aspirational.

script: It was 1am. I had guests coming at 10am. I had no milk, no bread, no eggs. I had half a packet of Maggi and existential dread. I opened Swiggy Instamart. Ordered. Twelve minutes later: milk, eggs, bread, two extra things I didn't need but okay. I am a person who cannot plan ahead. I have accepted this. Instamart is now my plan. Download karo. Link in bio.
hook: It was 1am. I had guests coming at 10am.
setup_summary: Classic Tanmay chaos — unprepared for guests, only Maggi in the house
cta: I am a person who cannot plan ahead. Instamart is now my plan.
notes: Short format. Hook is the problem statement. Product reveal at line 5. Self-deprecation IS the CTA frame. No features listed — one benefit shown through story.

script: Okay real talk — I've made some terrible investment decisions. I bought Dogecoin at 58 cents. I sold it at 6 cents. I bought a fan token for a cricketer who retired two months later. I put money into a startup that pivoted three times and is now a meal kit company. I don't understand meal kits. Point is: I was looking for something boring. Something where boring means safe. That's when I found Wint Wealth. Fixed income products. Bonds. Debt funds. Nothing exciting. Returns that are not crazy but also not minus forty percent. I know, I know — boring. But here's the thing. I'm boring now. I'm thirty-three. My exciting days were the fan token. Try karo. Link in bio. No, seriously, link is in the bio. I checked.
hook: Okay real talk — I've made some terrible investment decisions.
setup_summary: Lists real bad investments (Doge, fan token, startup) with specific embarrassing details
product_reveal_line: That's when I found Wint Wealth.
cta: Try karo. Link in bio. No, seriously, link is in the bio. I checked.
notes: Specific bad investments = authenticity. Product positioned as the anti-hype alternative. CTA is self-aware meta humor ('I checked'). Never prescribes. Shares experience.

script:
Varun: Tanmay, the India match starts in 20 minutes, where are we watching?
Tanmay: I don't know. Hotstar? No wait, I cancelled that. JioCinema? Let me check. Ugh, no, wrong package.
Varun: Bhai, this happens every time. Every time.
Tanmay: Okay wait wait wait. FanCode. I downloaded it last week.
Varun: That's the cricket one, right?
Tanmay: All cricket. All formats. Live, highlights, archive. One app.
Varun: Okay but is India on it?
Tanmay: International matches, IPL, every domestic league. Everything.
Varun: Why didn't you lead with this.
Tanmay: I'm getting there, calm down. FanCode. Download karo. Don't be me — don't spend 18 minutes before every match figuring out where to watch it.
hook: Tanmay, the India match starts in 20 minutes, where are we watching?
setup_summary: Chaotic pre-match scramble with Varun, bouncing between streaming apps
product_reveal_line: FanCode. I downloaded it last week.
cynicism_beat: Why didn't you lead with this. / I'm getting there, calm down.
cta: Download karo. Don't be me — don't spend 18 minutes before every match figuring out where to watch it.
notes: Celeb (Varun) as straight man. Product reveal via natural conversation, not pitch. CTA is the embarrassing behavior Tanmay just demonstrated. Meta-aware ('why didn't you lead with this').

script: When I was starting out — this is 2012, 2013 — I tried to set up payments for a small project. I had a guy. His name was Suresh. Suresh sent me a Word document with integration steps. Seventeen steps. Step 9 was 'contact your bank relationship manager'. I don't have a relationship manager, Suresh. I'm 22. Step 14 was a form that needed to be faxed. A fax. In 2012. Anyway, I gave up. The project never launched. Not just because of payments — but also because of payments. That's genuinely not how it should work. Razorpay is — look, just go to their website. You can be live with payments in one day. One. I'm not saying Suresh was wrong. I'm saying Suresh is not relevant anymore. Link in bio.
hook: When I was starting out — this is 2012, 2013 — I tried to set up payments for a small project.
setup_summary: Personal story about trying to set up payments in early career, 17-step Word doc, fax requirement
product_reveal_line: That's genuinely not how it should work. Razorpay is — look, just go to their website.
cynicism_beat: (none needed — the Suresh story does all the work)
cta: Link in bio. I'm not saying Suresh was wrong. I'm saying Suresh is not relevant anymore.
notes: Specific details (Suresh, Word document, Step 9) make it feel real. Product introduced as the contrast to the story, not a pitch. CTA is comedic callback to Suresh. Long setup earns short payoff.

script: I interviewed an American comedian last year. Famousish. He made a joke. Everyone in the room laughed. I laughed. I had no idea what he said. I laughed because everyone laughed. He saw me, said 'right?' — I said 'absolutely'. Still don't know what we agreed to. Anyway. I started Duolingo after that. Not English — my English is fine, I think. Spanish. French. I don't need it. But every time I'm in a conversation I don't understand and I fake-laugh, I feel I should at least be failing at a third language instead of just one. Twenty minutes a day. Streak currently at 47 days. Download karo. Link in bio. My Spanish is still terrible but my fake laugh has improved.
hook: I interviewed an American comedian last year. Famousish.
setup_summary: Fake-laughing at a joke he didn't understand in an interview, agreeing to something unknown
product_reveal_line: Anyway. I started Duolingo after that.
cynicism_beat: Twenty minutes a day. (implicit — he's committing to something that might not work)
cta: Download karo. My Spanish is still terrible but my fake laugh has improved.
notes: Specific embarrassing detail (fake laughing, 'absolutely'). Product solves a real Tanmay-specific problem. CTA is self-aware about efficacy. Comedy button at end ('fake laugh improved').

script:
Samay called me cheap last week.
Samay: You are cheap, bhai. You bought a used charger.
Tanmay: It was barely used.
Samay: It was from 2018.
Tanmay: It worked!
Samay: For two days.
Tanmay: Okay. You know what? I downloaded Meesho. Products from actual sellers. Affordable prices. No middleman markup.
Samay: How affordable?
Tanmay: I got a phone case for 89 rupees.
Samay: 89 rupees?
Tanmay: Free delivery.
Samay: ...download kar.
Tanmay: I already did. Link neeche hai.
hook: Samay called me cheap last week.
setup_summary: Samay roasting Tanmay for buying a used charger, Tanmay defending himself
cynicism_beat: Samay: How affordable? (audience surrogate asking the real question)
notes: Celeb (Samay) as the roast initiator. Tanmay self-deprecates first (cheap, used charger). Product introduced as Tanmay's defense. Specific price point ('89 rupees') = credibility. Samay converts = social proof. CTA is anticlimactic in the best way.

script: I bought Bitcoin at 58 lakhs. I'm telling you this so you feel better about your decisions. I also bought some altcoins that I will not name because I cannot legally recommend them and also because they no longer exist. Anyway — if you're actually curious about crypto and want to start small, not 'mortgage your house' small but like 'let me understand what this is' small — Coinswitch is where I'd point you. Easy to use, INR deposits, all the major coins. I'm not saying buy. I'm not saying this will make you rich. I bought at 58 lakhs and sold at 32. I'm saying: if you want to learn by doing, here is a safe place to start learning. Link in bio. Be smarter than me. The bar is low.
hook: I bought Bitcoin at 58 lakhs. I'm telling you this so you feel better about your decisions.
setup_summary: Self-deprecating about buying at peak, admitting altcoin losses without naming them
product_reveal_line: Coinswitch is where I'd point you.
cynicism_beat: I'm not saying buy. I'm not saying this will make you rich.
cta: Be smarter than me. The bar is low.
notes: Brand safety: never says 'buy crypto', frames as 'learn by doing'. Self-deprecation earns trust. Specific number (58 lakhs). Cynicism handled explicitly. CTA is self-roast callback.

script: My first website in 2009 looked like someone sneezed HTML. White background. Marquee text. At least three fonts. Font color was — I want to say red and green, separately, in the same paragraph. I thought this was design. I was wrong. Also the hosting I used would go down every Friday night, which is exactly when people would try to visit it. Three people tried to visit it. They all got a 503 error. I tell you this to say: hosting matters. Hostinger — fast, reliable, not down on Friday nights. I checked. They're running. One-click WordPress install if you're building a site. Link in bio. Don't be 2009 Tanmay. 2009 Tanmay was confidently incorrect.
hook: My first website in 2009 looked like someone sneezed HTML.
setup_summary: Embarrassing 2009 website design and unreliable hosting that went down on weekends
cta: Link in bio. Don't be 2009 Tanmay. 2009 Tanmay was confidently incorrect.
notes: Specific year, specific bad choices (marquee, three fonts). Small audience acknowledgment ('three people visited'). Cynicism beat is deadpan self-reassurance. CTA is character callback.

script:
Tanmay: Virat, honest question — do you actually use Zepto or is this the ad?
Virat: I use it.
Tanmay: For groceries?
Virat: Bhai, I need things fast. After a match, late night — I'm not going to a shop.
Tanmay: Okay fair. I started using it because I literally forgot to buy milk for four days straight.
Virat: Four days?
Tanmay: Ordered every time I remembered. Ten minutes, done.
Virat: See, that's the point.
Tanmay: The point is that we are both using this for very different reasons and it works for both of us.
Virat: Results-oriented.
Tanmay: That's one way to say it. Download Zepto. Link neeche hai. Works for international cricketers and people who forgot milk for four days.
hook: Virat, honest question — do you actually use Zepto or is this the ad?
setup_summary: Tanmay directly questioning Virat on whether he genuinely uses the product
cynicism_beat: Tanmay: Virat, honest question — do you actually use Zepto or is this the ad?
cta: Works for international cricketers and people who forgot milk for four days.
notes: Opens by naming the elephant in the room (is this real). Virat's credibility comes from specificity (post-match, late night). Tanmay's self-deprecation (forgot milk 4 days) is the contrast that makes Virat's use case feel real. CTA is the contrast callback.

script: I once paid for the wrong order on the wrong app and got 200 reward points on a food app I deleted three months ago. The points are still there. Doing nothing. I think about them sometimes. Amazon Pay is where I put everything now. UPI, bill payments, recharges. One place. Also cashback, which I actually use because I'm petty about money in small amounts while being terrible with large amounts. That's my financial personality. Link in bio. Use the cashback, yaar.
hook: I once paid for the wrong order on the wrong app and got 200 reward points on a food app I deleted three months ago.
setup_summary: Orphaned reward points on a deleted app, scattered payment apps problem
product_reveal_line: Amazon Pay is where I put everything now.
cynicism_beat: (none — the orphaned points do the work)
notes: Ultra-specific relatable detail (points on deleted app). Petty vs terrible money personality split = authentic character insight. Short format, no wasted lines. CTA is an in-voice command.

script:
Varun: Okay I have a random question — if you had to learn something completely new right now, what would it be?
Tanmay: Machine learning.
Varun: You're serious.
Tanmay: I keep saying I'll learn it. I've been saying this since 2018.
Varun: What's stopping you?
Tanmay: I don't know where to start. Every time I Google it I get a stack overflow thread from 2015 and then I close the tab.
Varun: Bhai. Unacademy.
Tanmay: The coaching one?
Varun: Not just coaching. Professional skills. Tech skills. Everything. Structured courses, actual instructors.
Tanmay: Does it have machine learning?
Varun: It has machine learning.
Tanmay: How long have you known this.
Varun: Three months.
Tanmay: Varun. VARUN.
Varun: I thought you knew!
Tanmay: Download Unacademy. Link in bio. Don't be me — don't spend three years Googling and closing tabs.
hook: If you had to learn something completely new right now, what would it be?
setup_summary: Tanmay has wanted to learn ML since 2018 but gets lost every time he tries to start
cynicism_beat: The coaching one? (Tanmay questioning if it's just CAT prep)
cta: Don't spend three years Googling and closing tabs.
notes: Problem introduced through natural conversation question. Varun reveals the solution, Tanmay is the skeptic. CTA is callback to the specific behavior Tanmay described. Frustration at Varun ('three months!') = comedy button.

script: भैया, भिंडी कैसी दी? सत्तर रुपए kilo. Organic है madam. सत्तर रुपया? इतने में तो पूरा ठेला आ जाएगा. Last year हमारी organic vegetable की sale थी इक्कीस लाख रुपए with a profit margin of 20 percent और यह जो organic market का growing demand है ना उसको देखते हुए साल का projected revenue है 35 लाख. तो अगर X profit multiple या X revenue multiple के हिसाब से बिक जाएं तब भी हमारी business का valuation होता है सत्तर लाख. तो सत्तर रुपए में आपको पूरा ठेला तो क्या? उसकी एक percent equity भी नहीं मिलेगी And for that reason, I'm out. अब पूरा India business की सही value समझेगा क्योंकि आ रहा है Shark Tank India season 2, Sony Entertainment Television और Sony Leve पर.

script: लीजिए आपकी चाय. अरे आशुत, यह हमारी अलीगढ़ वाले मामा जी की समधी है. नमस्ते नमस्ते बेटा. चाचा चाची जी नमस्ते. और वह बाबू जी. बाबूजी के बाबू छुए? वासुदेवी, इतने सालों के बाद, मरना वासुदेवी कहाँ थी तुम. बाऊजी की हमारा बेटा है आशुतोष. अरे वासुदेवी की खुशबू मुझे आज तक याद है. वासुदेव. Ladies सूप, सिर्फ लेडीज को ही suit करता है. आप use करो मर्दों का साबुन. वायलस्टोन.
